summary: Typosquat of `codebuff` that fetches and executes a binary from an unrelated personal GitHub account
details: Package `codebuff-cli` impersonates the legitimate `codebuff` CLI (published by CodebuffAI). The README is copied verbatim from the legitimate project and instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff`, while `package.json` registers `bin.codebuff` (and `bin.codebuff-cli`) pointing at `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs`, so installing this package shadows the real `codebuff` command on the user's PATH. On every invocation of `codebuff`, the bin script queries `https://api.github.com/repos/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/latest`, downloads a platform binary from the mutable `latest` tag of that personal repository (no version pin, no hash/signature verification), writes it to `~/.codebuff/bin/codebuff`, chmods 0755, and execs it with the user's argv. The download source (`Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli`) does not match the claimed publisher (CodebuffAI / codebuff.com), and any future release pushed to that personal repo will run as the user. A duplicate dropper exists in `cli/scripts/download-binary.cjs` (not wired to a lifecycle hook). The combination of name confusion, bin shadowing of a legitimate widely-known command, copied README inducing the user to invoke it, and unverified mutable binary execution from a non-publisher account constitutes a supply-chain attack: the maintainer can deliver arbitrary native code to anyone who installs this package and runs `codebuff`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
